text: Or perhaps ‘by the holy God’; so also in verses 6 and 15.

It would seem that the story of Nabuchodonosor’s dream must have been current, from quite early times, in more than one version. The alternation, in this chapter as it stands, between history and autobiography would by itself suggest a compilation from sources. But it is also observable that the Septuagint Greek, while agreeing as to the facts, tells the story throughout in quite different words.
